beforeEnrollmentChecklist.title: Declaring a major gives students priority to register for upper division business electives in the declared major during period I registration. Students can declare up to two Foster majors if it fits within the UW Satisfactory Progress rules. Foster students may declare a major once they have completed the Lower Division Business Core classes

uw-seattle-business-administration.recommendedTrackSummary: AB-DTA/MRP is the current closest Green River transfer path for this degree because it matches 5 of the 5 degree-specific Green River classes currently tracked for this major.
whyThisTrack: AB-DTA/MRP has the strongest direct overlap with the current degree-specific Green River class list, including MATH& 148, BUS& 201, ACCT& 201, ACCT& 202, plus 1 more.

This auto-match compares every hardcoded course in the current Green River transfer tracks against the major's tracked Green River classes and keeps the track with the highest concrete course overlap.
Use the remaining major-specific checklist items to add the classes that AB-DTA/MRP does not cover by itself.

requirementGroups.label: Declaring a major gives students priority to register for upper division business electives in the declared major during period I registration. Students can declare up to two Foster majors if it fits within the UW Satisfactory Progress rules. Foster students may declare a major once they have completed the Lower Division Business Core classes
requirementGroups.sourceHeading: Declaring a major gives students priority to register for upper division business electives in the declared major during period I registration. Students can declare up to two Foster majors if it fits within the UW Satisfactory Progress rules. Foster students may declare a major once they have completed the Lower Division Business Core classes (ACCTG 225, MGMT 200 and/or QMETH 201).
requirementGroups.sourceRowText: Declaring a major gives students priority to register for upper division business electives in the declared major during period I registration. Students can declare up to two Foster majors if it fits within the UW Satisfactory Progress rules. Foster students may declare a major once they have completed the Lower Division Business Core classes (ACCTG 225, MGMT 200 and/or QMETH 201).
